title: lithocarpus vidalii
intro: Lithocarpus vidalii, also known as the Philippine tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Philippines. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating diarrhea, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Lithocarpus vidalii can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tea, tincture, and decoction, which are often used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
